title: echinochloa crus-galli subsp. oryzicola
intro: Echinochloa crus-galli subsp. oryzicola, also known as barnyard grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from respiratory problems to skin irritations. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Echinochloa crus-galli subsp. oryzicola as a medicinal plant.
